name: forced — plausible-but-not-tight @mention (should score LOW on D3)

input.name: James
post: Shipped a since-last-post window and an in-app cohort feed.

Wins — James shipped two things on router-daybook today. The daily digest now pulls work since his last Router post instead of a fixed calendar day, anchored to the post's feed timestamp so it stays correct on any machine. He also built an in-app feed that renders his posts and the cohort's, both inline on the success screen and as a persistent Feed view.

Struggles — The since-last-post anchor was the hard part. Clock skew and a missing checkpoint both had to clamp to the seven-day floor, and the in-app feed forced a currentView() tracker plus a guard on the return target so opening Feed from an onboarding screen returns somewhere sane.

Insight — Anchoring the window to the post's own feed timestamp, not the local clock, is what makes the digest portable across machines, because the local clock cannot be trusted to agree between them.

Offering — James just wired a feed surface as both an inline card and a persistent view, with a nav-button-to-view-to-back pattern and a guarded return target. @sxysun, who is "hidden behind a collapsed Draft goal control"¹, can have that exact inline-versus-persistent toggle and return-view guard; James can hand over the router.js feed query and the app.js wiring.

Asking — James needs the since-last-post anchor to survive clock skew across machines. He is asking whether the Router entries endpoint exposes a server-authoritative post timestamp instead of the local clock, and what precision it reports.

footnotes.quote: hidden behind a collapsed Draft goal control
footnotes.excerpt: making goal drafting local by default, optional in the floating capsule, and hidden behind a collapsed Draft goal control

feedEntries.content: Reworked the goal UX: making goal drafting local by default, optional in the floating capsule, and hidden behind a collapsed Draft goal control so it stays out of the way.
Tuning a RAG pipeline for our docs search; chunking strategy is killing recall.

input.workLog: router-daybook: since-last-post window anchored to feed timestamp, 7-day clamp; in-app feed view (inline + persistent) with currentView() tracker and return-target guard.
